title: Hymn I.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: Seven Hymns on the Faith.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: On a certain day a pearl did I take up, my brethren; I saw in it mysteries pertaining to the Kingdom; semblances and types of the Majesty; it became a fountain, and I drank out of it mysteries of the Son.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: I put it, my brethren, upon the palm of my hand, that I might examine it: I went to look at it on one side, and it proved faces on all sides.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: I found out that the Son was incomprehensible, since He is wholly Light.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: In its brightness I beheld the Bright One Who cannot be clouded, and in its pureness a great mystery, even the Body of our Lord which is well-refined: in its undividedness I saw the Truth which is undivided.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: It was so that I saw there its pure conception,—the Church, and the Son within her.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: The cloud was the likeness of her that bare Him, and her type the heaven, since there shone forth from her His gracious Shining.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: I saw therein His trophies, and His victories, and His crowns.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: I saw His helpful and overflowing graces, and His hidden things with His revealed things.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: It was greater to me than the ark, for I was astonied thereat: I saw therein folds without shadow to them because it was a daughter of light, types vocal without tongues, utterances of mysteries without lips, a silent harp that without voice gave out melodies.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: The trumpet falters and the thunder mutters; be not thou daring then; leave things hidden, take things revealed.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: Thou hast seen in the clear sky a second shower; the clefts of thine ears, as from the clouds, they are filled with interpretations.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: And as that manna which alone filled the people, in the place of pleasant meats, with its pleasantnesses, so does this pearl fill me in the place of books, and the reading thereof, and the explanations thereof.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: And when I asked if there were yet other mysteries, it had no mouth for me that I might hear from, neither any ears wherewith it might hear me.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: O thou thing without senses, whence I have gained new senses!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: It answered me and said, “The daughter of the sea am I, the illimitable sea!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: And from that sea whence I came up it is that there is a mighty treasury of mysteries in my bosom!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: Search thou out the sea, but search not out the Lord of the sea!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: “I have seen the divers who came down after me, when astonied, so that from the midst of the sea they returned to the dry ground; for a few moments they sustained it not.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: Who would linger and be searching on into the depths of the Godhead?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: “The waves of the Son are full of blessings, and with mischiefs too.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: Have ye not seen, then, the waves of the sea, which if a ship should struggle with them would break her to pieces, and if she yield herself to them, and rebel not against them, then she is preserved?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: In the sea all the Egyptians were choked, though they scrutinised it not, and, without prying, the Hebrews too were overcome upon the dry land, and how shall ye be kept alive?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: And the men of Sodom were licked up by the fire, and how shall ye prevail?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: “At these uproars the fish in the sea were moved, and Leviathan also.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: Have ye then a heart of stone that ye read these things and run into these errors?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: O great fear that justice also should be so long silent!”

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: “Searching is mingled with thanksgiving, and whether of the two will prevail?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: The incense of praise riseth along with the fume of disputation from the tongue, and unto which shall we hearken?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: Prayer and prying [come] from one mouth, and which shall we listen to?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: “For three days was Jonah a neighbour [of mine] in the sea: the living things that were in the sea were affrighted, [saying,] “Who shall flee from God?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: The Pearl. Seven Hymns on the Faith.
verses.text: Jonah fled, and ye are obstinate at your scrutiny of Him!”
